title: Mujadara

overview.description: A Levantine dish of lentils and rice topped with deeply caramelised onions and served with cool yogurt.

method: Rinse the lentils thoroughly.
Place the lentils in a saucepan with the water or vegetable stock, cinnamon stick and bay leaves.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Cook for 20–25 minutes until the lentils are beginning to soften but still retain their shape.
While the lentils cook, heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based saucepan or frying pan.
Add the sliced onions.
Cook over medium-low heat, stirring regularly, until deeply golden brown and caramelised.
This may take 30–40 minutes.
Remove about half of the onions and set aside for the topping.
Add the cumin, ground coriander and black pepper to the remaining onions.
Cook for 30 seconds.
Drain the lentils if necessary, reserving their cooking liquid.
Add the rice to the pan with the onions and spices.
Add the partially cooked lentils.
Add enough cooking liquid to give approximately 450ml total liquid.
Add the salt.
Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce the heat to very low and cook until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Turn off the heat and leave covered for 10 minutes.
Remove the cinnamon stick and bay leaves.
Fluff the rice and lentils gently with a fork.
Top generously with the reserved caramelised onions.
Serve with plain yogurt.

learning.did-you-know: Mujadara is one of the great examples of how simple staple ingredients can form a complete and satisfying meal. The dish has many regional variations across the Levant.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to caramelise onions slowly and how to coordinate the cooking of lentils and rice so they finish with the right texture.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A cheap, filling and genuinely delicious Pantry Monday meal that makes excellent use of your lentil and rice supplies.
chapman-tips: The onions are the heart of the dish, so don't rush them.
The best mujadara has onions that are deeply golden and sweet rather than merely softened.
Green lentils are particularly suitable because they retain their shape.
The lentils should be partially cooked before the rice is added.
Avoid stirring the rice too much once it begins cooking.
Plain yogurt provides a useful cool, creamy contrast to the sweet onions and earthy lentils.
The dish can be served warm or at room temperature.
A little lemon juice in the yogurt or a sharp salad alongside works very well.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze in portions once completely cooled.
notes.leftovers: Reheat with a splash of water. The caramelised onions are best stored separately where possible so they retain more texture.
